flat_day.quote: Every bestseller had a zero-sale day. The authors who made it kept showing up anyway.
Quiet days aren't failure. They're the pause before the next chapter.
A flat day is data, not destiny. Tomorrow's numbers don't know today's numbers.
The story isn't over. This is just the scene where the protagonist catches their breath.

ad_spend_fear.quote: Spending money to make money is terrifying — until it works. Trust the test.
Every ad dollar is a question. The answer comes in the data, not the worry.
You're not losing money. You're buying information about what your readers respond to.
Short-term spend for long-term readership is a trade most authors never have the courage to make.

small_wins.quote: Small numbers are still numbers. You earned those readers one by one.
Momentum doesn't start at the top. It starts exactly here.
Ten readers who love your book are worth more than a thousand who never heard of it.
These numbers are proof you've begun. That's the hardest part of any story.

we_see_you.quote: You uploaded your data. You showed up. That's the whole game.
Tracking your numbers is an act of professionalism. Most authors never get here.
You're building a publishing business one month at a time. That takes guts.
The authors who win aren't always the most talented — they're the ones who keep measuring and adjusting.
